text.content:
Items generated by Quartermaster start masked. Players see mundane names, generic icons, and hidden rarity until you identify the item.

What gets masked

Three toggles in Configure Identification control what is hidden:

  • Obscure Consumables - potions and oils appear as "Sealed Vial".
  • Obscure Spell Scrolls - scrolls appear as "Unidentified Scroll".
  • Obscure Magical Items - weapons, armor, and wondrous gear appear as their mundane base type.

Turn any toggle off if you want that category to show true names immediately.

How to identify

When GM-Only Identification is on (default), only you can reveal items. Players cannot flip the toggle on item sheets.

  • Item sheet wand - open the item sheet and click the identification wand.
  • Respite integration - if Ionrift Respite is installed, players can pick Identify as a camp activity during a long rest to self-serve.

What identification reveals

The original name, icon, rarity, price, magical bonus, attunement requirement, and full description are restored. The item becomes fully functional on the character sheet.

Cursed items

Quartermaster ships with a pool of SRD cursed items. In base Quartermaster, these are standard items with their published descriptions - identification reveals them normally. Potions of Poison are not concealed.

For the full cursed item lifecycle - forging custom curses, concealed lure properties, attunement triggers, curse activation, sticky removal, and disguised Potions of Poison - install Ionrift Cursewright.
